mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to equity cash flows, directly increasing the present value of S&P 500 earnings and producing positive forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 4.0% within a single month, it signals a rapid deterioration in labor market conditions that triggers recession fears, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or the predictive Rank IC drops below 0.02 over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: The joint contraction of industrial production and residential housing starts indicates a synchronized demand-supply shock that erodes corporate profit margins, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to predict negative returns in more than 50% of out-of-sample instances, or the interaction term in a multivariate regression shows a p-value > 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-inflation regime where CPI exceeds 3.5%, an increase in real consumer credit reflects aggressive household borrowing that eventually crowds out corporate investment and triggers equity sell-offs over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: high-inflation environment with elevated consumer credit
failure_condition: Forward returns turn positive when CPIAUCSL exceeds 3.5% and RSAFS rises, or the signal's directional accuracy falls below 55% over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real GDP indicates expanding aggregate demand that directly boosts corporate revenues and earnings, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains negative or near zero over any 3-year rolling window, or forward returns do not significantly outperform the baseline in GDP growth quarters.

mechanism_narrative: When nonfarm payrolls decline by more than 50,000 in a single month, it signals a sudden labor market contraction that precedes earnings downgrades, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal fails to predict negative returns in > 50% of out-of-sample instances, or the forward return distribution shows no significant left skew during triggered periods.

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous rise in the federal funds rate and a declining industrial production index indicates that monetary tightening is actively suppressing economic activity, which compresses equity multiples and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns in more than 50% of instances, or the interaction term in a predictive model yields a statistically insignificant coefficient (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: During periods of tight labor markets where unemployment stays below 4.0%, a sustained increase in industrial production reflects supply-side capacity expansion that eventually outpaces demand, leading to margin compression and negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
regime_hint: tight labor market with supply expansion
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive when INDPRO rises and UNRATE stays below 4.0%, or the predictive power vanishes in post-2020 data.
